seoTitle: How to Erase Part of a Graphic in Canva
metaDescription: Learn how to erase part of a graphic in Canva using the built-in eraser, ideal for cleaning up images or customizing elements.
intro: Sometimes a graphic or photo needs a small section removed — whether it's a distracting background detail or part of a shape you don't need. Canva's eraser tool makes this possible without switching to a separate photo editor. This guide covers using it effectively.

steps.heading: Select the image or graphic
steps.body: Click on the element you want to edit so its toolbar options appear.

steps.heading: Open the Edit Image or Eraser tool
steps.body: Look for 'Edit image' in the toolbar, then select the Eraser option from the menu that appears.

steps.heading: Adjust the brush size
steps.body: Set the eraser brush to an appropriate size for the area you're removing — smaller for detail work, larger for broad areas.

steps.heading: Erase the unwanted section
steps.body: Click and drag over the part of the image you want to remove.

steps.heading: Review and refine
steps.body: Zoom in to check the edges of your erased area and touch up any spots you missed.

faq.question: Is the eraser tool free in Canva?
faq.answer: Basic erasing is available on the free plan, though some advanced background removal features are part of Canva Pro.

faq.question: Can I undo an erase if I remove too much?
faq.answer: Yes, you can undo the action or use the restore brush, if available, to bring back erased areas.

faq.question: Does erasing part of an image reduce its quality?
faq.answer: No, erasing only affects the visible area — it doesn't compress or degrade the rest of the image.

faq.question: Can I erase part of a shape as well as a photo?
faq.answer: The eraser tool is primarily designed for photos and graphics; for shapes, you'd typically use overlapping shapes to hide part of it instead.
